title: Getting started with anatomogramdata
text: Call arguments see ’s available — every tissue organ system(s) belongs :
code: head(anatomogram_select("male")) #>    tissue_id tissue_name           system #> 1 CL_0000084        <NA> Lymphatic/Immune #> 2 CL_0000233        <NA>   Cardiovascular #> 3 CL_0000236        <NA> Lymphatic/Immune #> 4 CL_0000576        <NA> Lymphatic/Immune #> 5 CL_0000623        <NA> Lymphatic/Immune #> 6 CL_0000738   leukocyte Lymphatic/Immune unique(anatomogram_select("male")$system) #>  [1] "Lymphatic/Immune" "Cardiovascular"   "Respiratory"      "Endocrine"        #>  [5] "Integumentary"    "Digestive"        "Reproductive"     "Nervous"          #>  [9] "Muscular"         "Urinary"          "Skeletal"

what: Organ mode: select specific organs, optionally with values
title: Getting started with anatomogramdata
text: Pass organs character vector. element matched either literal ontology ID (e.g. "UBERON_0000948") fuzzily (case-insensitive, partial) tissue name. Name element attach value — useful fill:  Unnamed elements pure selection, value attached: organ name matches zero tissues warns dropped rather failing whole call; name matches one distinct tissue (e.g. "hippocampus", left right entry separate ontology IDs) error listing candidates, since ambiguity needs decision make, one package guess .
code: d <- anatomogram_select("male", organs = c(kidney = "High", heart = "Low"))  ggplot(d, aes(x, y, group = group)) +   geom_polygon(data = subset(d, tissue_id == "outline"),                fill = "grey90", colour = "black") +   geom_polygon(data = subset(d, tissue_id != "outline"),                aes(fill = value), colour = "black") +   coord_fixed() +   theme_void() selection_only <- anatomogram_select("male", organs = c("kidney", "heart"))

what: System mode: pull whole organ systems
title: Getting started with anatomogramdata
text: Pass system instead organs select every tissue belonging one organ systems (Skeletal, Digestive, Nervous, — see [organ_systems] full list). outline included automatically:  Requesting one system facet-ready — tissue belonging one requested system (pancreas, Digestive Endocrine) correctly appears per system:  organs system mutually exclusive single call — specifying error. Call anatomogram_select() twice rbind() results genuinely need .
code: bones <- anatomogram_select("male", system = "Skeletal")  ggplot(bones, aes(x, y, group = group)) +   geom_polygon(data = subset(bones, tissue_id == "outline"),                fill = "grey95", colour = "black") +   geom_polygon(data = subset(bones, tissue_id != "outline"),                fill = "grey40", colour = "black") +   coord_fixed() +   theme_void() several <- anatomogram_select("male", system = c("Digestive", "Endocrine"))  ggplot(several, aes(x, y, group = group)) +   geom_polygon(data = subset(several, tissue_id == "outline"),                fill = "grey95", colour = "black") +   geom_polygon(data = subset(several, tissue_id != "outline"),                fill = "steelblue", colour = "black") +   facet_wrap(~ system) +   coord_fixed() +   theme_void()

what: The outline: silhouette vs. interior contour lines
title: Getting started with anatomogramdata
text: hgMale/hgFemale’s outline_role column splits body outline three roles, computed geometry rather tag source SVG (isn’t one): "silhouette" (plain outer body shape), "contour_detail" (shape interior finger/toe/joint lines), "feature" (small facial marks). Filter just roles want:
code: outline <- hgMale[hgMale$tissue_id == "outline", ] clean_silhouette <- outline[outline$outline_role == "silhouette", ]  ggplot(clean_silhouette, aes(x, y, group = group)) +   geom_polygon(fill = "grey90", colour = "black") +   coord_fixed() +   theme_void()

title: Getting started with anatomogramdata
text: anatomogram_select() covers common cases, hgMale/hgFemale already carry id/group columns pre-flipped y ggplot2::geom_map() needs map argument directly — useful mapping one aesthetic real data (e.g. fill linewidth together). See examples/geom_map_demo.R examples/cause_of_death_demo.R package source worked patterns.

title: Getting started with anatomogramdata
text: bundled data parsed directly EBI Expression Atlas anatomogram source (Apache-2.0 code / CC--4.0 images) — see citation("anatomogramdata") cite package underlying source.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Peter Tanksley. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Tanksley P (2026). anatomogramdata: Tidy Human Anatomogram Polygon Data EBI Expression Atlas Source. R package. Parsing pipeline structurally indebted gganatogram package (Jesper Grud Skat Madsen / jespermaag) prior art, built independently live EBI source rather fork code bundled data., https://github.com/petertanksley/anatomogramdata. EBI Gene Expression Group (2026). “Expression Atlas anatomogram source (human).” Images CC--4.0, code Apache-2.0. package's hgMale/hgFemale/organ_systems data parsed directly source – cite alongside package citation , since CC--4.0 license underlying images requires attribution., https://github.com/ebi-gene-expression-group/anatomogram.
code: @Manual{,   title = {anatomogramdata: Tidy Human Anatomogram Polygon Data from the EBI Expression Atlas Source},   author = {Peter Tanksley},   year = {2026},   url = {https://github.com/petertanksley/anatomogramdata},   note = {R package. Parsing pipeline structurally indebted to the gganatogram package (Jesper Grud Skat Madsen / jespermaag) as prior art, but built independently against the live EBI source rather than as a fork of its code or bundled data.}, } @Misc{,   title = {Expression Atlas anatomogram source (human)},   author = {{EBI Gene Expression Group}},   year = {2026},   url = {https://github.com/ebi-gene-expression-group/anatomogram},   note = {Images CC-BY-4.0, code Apache-2.0. This package's hgMale/hgFemale/organ_systems data is parsed directly from this source -- cite it alongside the package citation above, since the CC-BY-4.0 license on the underlying images requires attribution.}, }

what: Tidy Human Anatomogram Polygon Data from the EBI Expression Atlas Source
title: Tidy Human Anatomogram Polygon Data from the EBI Expression Atlas Source
text: Tidy, ontology-tagged, ggplot2-ready human anatomogram polygon data (hgMale/hgFemale), parsed directly EBI Expression Atlas anatomogram SVG source — frozen hardcoded snapshot. data package, plotting package. custom geom plotting function — plot bundled data ggplot2::geom_map() ggplot2::geom_polygon() directly, already provide full aesthetic flexibility (fill, alpha, colour, linewidth, …) package-specific code needed. Human (male + female) — mouse/-organism data.

title: Tidy Human Anatomogram Polygon Data from the EBI Expression Atlas Source
Tidy Human Anatomogram Polygon Data from the EBI Expression Atlas Source
text: anatomogram_select() package’s single entry point browsing, selecting, filtering bundled anatomy data. See examples/ worked patterns, including faceting organ system ggplot2::geom_map() power-user path mapping one aesthetic .
code: library(anatomogramdata) library(ggplot2)  # browse: what tissues and organ systems are available anatomogram_select("male")  # select specific organs and attach a value to each (e.g. for fill) d <- anatomogram_select("male", organs = c(kidney = "High", heart = "Low"))  ggplot(d, aes(x, y, group = group)) +   geom_polygon(data = subset(d, tissue_id == "outline"),                fill = "grey90", colour = "black") +   geom_polygon(data = subset(d, tissue_id != "outline"),                aes(fill = value), colour = "black") +   coord_fixed() +   theme_void()  # or pull a whole organ system at once bones <- anatomogram_select("male", system = "Skeletal")

title: Tidy Human Anatomogram Polygon Data from the EBI Expression Atlas Source
text: Built directly EBI Expression Atlas anatomogram source (Apache-2.0 code / CC--4.0 images), fork gganatogram package’s code bundled data — though structurally indebted gganatogram (Jesper Grud Skat Madsen / jespermaag) prior art. See dev/NOTES.md full build history attribution/licensing details.

title: Tidy Human Anatomogram Polygon Data from the EBI Expression Atlas Source

what: Browse, select, or filter anatomogram tissues – by organ, by organ system — anatomogram_select
title: Browse, select, or filter anatomogram tissues – by organ, by organ system — anatomogram_select
text: single entry point three things otherwise need separate functions : browsing tissues/systems exist, pulling one whole organ systems, selecting specific organs (optionally attaching value , e.g. fill). Output always shaped ggplot2::geom_polygon() directly (see examples/anatomogram_values_demo.R, examples/anatomogram_system_demo.R) – geom_map() join needed.

title: Browse, select, or filter anatomogram tissues – by organ, by organ system — anatomogram_select
code: anatomogram_select(   sex = c("male", "female"),   organs = NULL,   system = NULL,   value_name = "value",   outline = TRUE )

title: Browse, select, or filter anatomogram tissues – by organ, by organ system — anatomogram_select
text: sex "male" "female". organs NULL (default), character vector selecting specific organs. element either literal tissue_id (e.g. "UBERON_0000948") matched fuzzily (case-insensitive, partial) tissue_name. element matching zero tissues emits warning() dropped; one matching one distinct tissue_id (e.g. "hippocampus", left right entry separate IDs) error listing candidates – use specific term literal tissue_id. Naming element (e.g. c(kidney = "high")) attaches value value_name; unnamed elements pure selection. Mutually exclusive system. system NULL (default), character vector one organ systems (see organ_systems full list, call anatomogram_select(sex) organs/system NULL browse). Unknown names error listing valid ones. Mutually exclusive organs. value_name Name column holding attached values organ mode (default "value"). Ignored browse/system mode. outline TRUE (default), include body outline rows (system mode, per requested system block), result can drive outline layer filled-organs layer.

title: Browse, select, or filter anatomogram tissues – by organ, by organ system — anatomogram_select
text: organs system NULL (browse mode): lookup data frame tissue_id, tissue_name, system (tissue one system appears one row). Otherwise: usual hgMale/hgFemale polygon columns filtered selection, plus system column (system mode) value_name column (organ mode).

what: Human male tissue polygon data — hgMale
title: Human male tissue polygon data — hgMale
text: tidy tibble body-outline organ polygons human male anatogram, parsed directly EBI Expression Atlas anatomogram SVG source (see data-raw/build_data.R dev/NOTES.md).

title: Human male tissue polygon data — hgMale
Human male tissue polygon data — hgMale
text: tibble one row per polygon vertex: tissue_id Ontology ID (e.g. "UBERON_0000948"), "outline" body silhouette. tissue_name Human-readable tissue name SVG's <title> element, present (NA handful cell-type-marker tissues – see dev/NOTES.md). ontology Ontology prefix ("UBERON" "CL"), NA outline. element_index, subpath Internal grouping keys – use polygon_id instead unless debugging parser. polygon_id, group Identical, duplicated two names: unique ID per disjoint polygon ring. Use directly group passing data ggplot2::geom_map()'s map argument (see examples/geom_map_demo.R) – geom_map() requires literal group column, without , multi-blob organs (e.g. two lungs) incorrectly merge one connect--dots shape. id Duplicate tissue_id, present ggplot2::geom_map() requires literal id column map argument. x, y Vertex coordinates, already oriented right-side-direct plotting – add ggplot2::scale_y_reverse(). (raw SVG source y growing downward; y already flipped , package-data build time, rather asking every plot reverse – scale_y_reverse() actively breaks ggplot2::geom_map(), since geom_map()'s polygon coordinates never pass normal scale-training pipeline. See dev/NOTES.md, Milestone 8.) outline_role tissue_id == "outline" rows (NA otherwise): "silhouette" (plain outer body shape), "contour_detail" (full-body shape interior cut-lines fingers/toes/ears/joints), "feature" (small facial marks – eyes, nostril, mouth). Classified geometry build time (bounding-box area, point count), tag SVG source – see dev/NOTES.md, Milestone 11.

title: Human male tissue polygon data — hgMale
text: https://github.com/ebi-gene-expression-group/anatomogram (images CC--4.0, code Apache-2.0). See dev/NOTES.md attribution parsing details.

what: Organ system membership for anatomogram tissues — organ_systems
title: Organ system membership for anatomogram tissues — organ_systems
text: hand-curated lookup table mapping tissue_id hgMale/hgFemale one organ systems (Skeletal, Muscular, Integumentary, Nervous, Endocrine, Cardiovascular, Lymphatic/Immune, Respiratory, Digestive, Urinary, Reproductive). Nothing EBI SVG source encodes – see data-raw/organ_systems.csv dev/NOTES.md, Milestone 11, curation approach. Use anatomogram_select() rather joining table directly common case.

title: Organ system membership for anatomogram tissues — organ_systems
Organ system membership for anatomogram tissues — organ_systems
text: data frame, long format (one row per tissue_id x system pair – tissue belonging one system, e.g. pancreas Digestive Endocrine, one row): tissue_id Ontology ID, matches hgMale/hgFemale's tissue_id. tissue_name Human-readable name, readability – used matching (tissue_name strings inconsistent across sexes tissue_id; join tissue_id instead). system One 11 systems listed .
